title: unshare: Fix running errors in non-privileged container
body:
When running buildah in a non-privileged container in k8s, the following error occurs:

```
root# buildah images
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
ERRO[0000] error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax
ERRO[0000] (unable to determine exit status)
```

This patch is to fix this error.

Signed-off-by: Zhou Hao <zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com>

comments.body: @giuseppe PTAL
Build/Tests issues hopefully fixed with #1020
@giuseppe PTAL

why is `unshare` failing?  Is the kernel too old?

The tests we are dropping are valid ones, are you able to build a container once you drop these tests in a container?  It'd probably fail on `buildah run` if you haven't `CAP_SYS_ADMIN`

> Is the kernel too old?

Output of uname -a:
```
Linux zz-ubuntu-78f657b855-ljrx8 3.10.0-862.11.6.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Tue Aug 14 21:49:04 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
> It'd probably fail on buildah run if you haven't CAP_SYS_ADMIN

Only this one command can't be executed, but other commands can still be executed.

> are you able to build a container once you drop these tests in a container?

This modification guarantees that other commands can be executed, but the same problem will occur with the run command. I wonder if there are other solutions?

> This modification guarantees that other commands can be executed, but the same problem will occur with the run command. I wonder if there are other solutions?

could you try to use the chroot isolation?  Does that work?

>could you try to use the chroot isolation? Does that work?


I tried it and it still went wrong.
```
root@zz-ubuntu-78f657b855-ljrx8:~# buildah run --isolation=chroot 747d sh
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
                                                     error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax
```

I run into the same problem with my fedora 28 machine and Kernel `4.18.9-200.fc28.x86_64`
Isolation `rootless` and `chroot` throw the same error

```
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
time="2018-09-27T09:01:10Z" level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
time="2018-09-27T09:01:10Z" level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
```
using the chroot isolation and having this patch solve the issue?
In any case, CAP_SYS_ADMIN is required to setup the environment for launching a container.  If user namespaces are not available, you'll probably need a privileged container

With the fix I don't run into the error. Buildah starts in a container but then exits with 
```
error running container: error creating new mount namespace for [/bin/sh -c apt-get update     && apt-get install -y curl unzip procps     && apt-get clean autoclean     && apt-get autoremove -y     && rm -rf /var/lib/{apt,dpkg,cache,log}/]: operation not permitted
```
This only works if I start buildah with `buildah --storage-driver=vfs bud --isolation=rootless` and a hostmount into the container.

Thanks @rhatdan . --isolation chroot still gives me
`Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted`
That happens because the container has not enough capabilities and chroot as well needs them.  You should add CAP_SYS_ADMIN.

@giuseppe I think chroot was created to not need CAP_SYS_ADMIN so we can run buildah inside of locked down containers.  I believe @nalind does this.

@q384566678 Looks like the tests passed?
@q384566678 Were  you able to get buildah to run in k8s with --isolation  chroot without having to add SYS_ADMIN?  If yes can we close this PR.

> Were you able to get buildah to run in k8s with --isolation chroot without having to add SYS_ADMIN?

@rhatdan No, running the following command without modifying the code will still cause an error.

```
root@zz-ubuntu-78f657b855-ljrx8:~# buildah run --isolation=chroot 747d sh
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
ERRO[0000] error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax 
ERRO[0000] (unable to determine exit status) 
```

So buildah is always attempting to create a USERNS even when root.
It should definitely not do that.
@giuseppe is this true ^^

it creates the userns if it doesn't have enough capabilities.  Even the chroot isolation needs these capabilities to configure the `podman run` environment.  Creating an user namespace gives CAP_SYS_ADMIN limited to that namespace.  So either userNS are supported or the container should already have enough capabilities that a new userNS is not required.
@giuseppe So you reject this PR?
yes, I think it won't solve the problem of running buildah in a non-privileged container when user namespaces are not available.

I am getting the same error trying to build as a non priveleged user on a debian box, is there a way to grant this capability to a user?:
```bash
~| uname -a
Linux blowpop 4.17.0-3-amd64 #1 SMP Debian 4.17.17-1 (2018-08-18) x86_64 GNU/Linux
~| buildah -v
buildah version 1.5-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
~| buildah from ${1:-fedora}
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Operation not permitted
ERRO[0000] error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax 
ERRO[0000] (unable to determine exit status)
```

@lollipopman on Ubuntu/Debian userNS for unprivileged users are disabled by default.  You need to enable user namespaces for unprivileged users with (as root):

`echo 1 > /proc/sys/kernel/unprivileged_userns_clone`.
@giuseppe thanks, that fixed it for me, it would be great if buildah could detect whether they are enabled and issue a better error message.
@lollipopman Great idea.  Also we should update the man pages, and installation docs.
@lollipopman Could you open an Issue to fix this.

>yes, I think it won't solve the problem of running buildah in a non-privileged container when user namespaces are not available.

So this is still an unresolved issue, we need to find a way to solve this problem.
@rhatdan issue created, https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/1068
Thanks @lollipopman
@q384566678 There is no way for a non priv user to create multiple UIDs without UserNS.  I think some tools have faked this out, but I am not sure it is worth hacking on this, since we have UserNS>

is this issue resolved?
I am still getting same error on Openshift4 and Jenkins with below build command 

buildah bud --device /dev/fuse --isolation=chroot runtime=crun --format=oci --tls-verify=true --layers -f ${WORKSPACE}/Dockerfile .

Error
time="2021-07-27T07:06:44Z" level=warning msg="error reading allowed ID mappings: error reading subuid mappings for user \"1000200000\" and subgid mappings for group \"1000200000\": No subuid ranges found for user \"1000200000\" in /etc/subuid"
time="2021-07-27T07:06:44Z" level=warning msg="Found no UID ranges set aside for user \"1000200000\" in /etc/subuid."
time="2021-07-27T07:06:44Z" level=warning msg="Found no GID ranges set aside for user \"1000200000\" in /etc/subgid."
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
time="2021-07-27T07:06:49Z" level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
time="2021-07-27T07:06:49Z" level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
Looks like openshift needs to be loosened up to run builds.  You might want to look at the openshift builder.  It uses Buildah under the covers.
